seoTitle: How to Add a Signature in Zendesk (2026 Guide)
metaDescription: Set up a professional email signature in Zendesk for both individual agents and the whole team, so every ticket reply looks consistent and on-brand.
intro: A missing or inconsistent email signature can make support replies feel unfinished and unprofessional. This guide covers setting up a personal signature from your Zendesk profile as well as configuring a global signature template from the Admin Center so every agent's replies carry the right branding.

steps.heading: Set your personal signature
steps.body: Open your individual user profile in Zendesk Support and find the signature box in the left sidebar to enter your name, title, and contact details.

steps.heading: Apply basic formatting
steps.body: Use Zendesk's Markdown controls to add bold text, italics, and clickable links to your signature.

steps.heading: Access the global signature settings
steps.body: As an admin, open the Admin Center and go to the agent interface settings to find the common signature configuration.

steps.heading: Insert dynamic placeholders
steps.body: Add template placeholders in the common signature box so it fills in each agent's name and details automatically.

steps.heading: Handle multiple brands
steps.body: If your organization supports several brands, set up a distinct signature for each one to keep customer-facing communication consistent.

steps.heading: Test the output
steps.body: Open a public comment on a test ticket to confirm the signature spacing, links, and formatting render as expected.

faq.question: Can each agent customize their own signature?
faq.answer: Yes, agents can edit their personal signature from their individual profile settings, in addition to any global template an admin has configured.

faq.question: Does Zendesk support HTML signatures?
faq.answer: Zendesk signatures primarily use Markdown formatting rather than raw HTML, so stick to bold, italics, and links for reliable rendering.

faq.question: Will the signature appear on internal notes too?
faq.answer: Signatures are designed for customer-facing public replies rather than internal team notes.

faq.question: Can I preview my signature before it goes live?
faq.answer: Yes, sending a test reply on a sample ticket is the easiest way to confirm your signature looks right before relying on it.
